rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. MARTINA QUINN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default [Ccp 473]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 20, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANTS MARTINA QUINN, MIKHAIL AFENDIKOV Motion For Relief From Default [CCP 473]. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' ATTORNEYS' HAVE PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE OF EXCUSABLE NEGLECT TO WARRANT RELIEF. HOWEVER, THE COURT DIRECTS PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PRESENT THE COURT WITH AN ATTORNEY FEE REQUEST IN THAT THE COURT WILL ORDER THAT DEFENDANTS' BE REQUIRED TO PAY COSTS OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S WORK ON THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA MANGAN VS. KEY MORTGAGE CORP DBA KMC MORTGAGE CORP
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 20, 2011, line 5, DEFENDANT MORTGAGEIT, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (breach of Implied Covenant) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not plead any facts to support the claim and a lender does not owe a fiduciary duty to a borrower. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 3rd, and 7th causes of action (Deceit and Concealment) is overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled these claims. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 4th cause of action (UCL) is likewise overruled. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 5th cause of action (Promissory Estoppel) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not allege a ¿clear and ambiguous¿ promise as required by U.S. Ecology Inc. v. State of California, (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 887, 901. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 6th cause of action (Intentional Misrepresentation) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts with more particularity to support its claim. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 8th cause of action (Unjust Enrichment) is sustained without leave to amend. This is not a cause of action. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 9th cause of action (Quiet Title) is sustained without leave to amend. This requires adverse claims to title and the Amended Complaint indicates that this Defendant no longer claims an interest. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 10th cause of action (Rosenthal Act) is sustained without leave to amend. There are no facts that show how Defendant is a debt collector or that what it did was improper. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 11th cause of action (Civil Conspiracy) is sustained without leave to amend. This is not an independent cause of action. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 12th cause of action (Declaratory Relief) is sustained without leave to amend. There is no existing controversy between the parties as Plaintiff admits that this defendant no longer has an interest in the loan. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 13t

rulings.Case Title: NED J. HONG et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 20, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., VINCENT CHOWS'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st, 2nd, 5th, 6th and 7th causes of action is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim against defendants. Moreover, while Defendant Bank of America had no duty to Plaintiffs pursuant to the amount of the loan and Plaintiffs¿ ability to pay the loan, it had a duty to fairly disclose information about the nature and terms of the loan. It also had a duty to ensure that teller transactions were not fraudulently conducted. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 3rd and 4th causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot seek both rescission and Cancellation of the instrument (both cancel the instrument and keep the funds). Moreover, plaintiff must offer to restore the benefit received from Bank of America in order to seek rescission of the loan. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: BAYSIDE BOATWORKS, INC. VS. BRIAN RANDOLPH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 20, 2011, line 12, DEFENDANTS BRIAN RANDOLPH, SAN FRANCISCO INSURANCE CENTER, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action (breach of Fiduciary Duty) is sustained without leave to amend. A broker¿s duties to his/her client are defined by negligence law. Workmen¿s Auto Ins. Co. v. Guy Carpenter & Co. Inc., (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 14. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 5th cause of action (Fraud) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts with sufficient particularity to the support this claim. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 6th cause of action (Indemnity) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to plead further facts regarding the implied indemnification. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 7th cause of action (Contribution and Declaratory Relief) is sustained without leave to amend as to both. Contribution is a right that comes into existence once a judgment is issued declaring multiple defendants jointly liable to the plaintiff. Here there has been no such judgment. Declaratory Relief is also inapplicable in this context. The negligence and breach of contract causes of action will address issues involving future claims head on. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: BAYSIDE BOATWORKS, INC. VS. BRIAN RANDOLPH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 20, 2011, line 12, DEFENDANT BRIAN RANDOLPH, SAN FRANCISCO INSURANCE CENTER, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The Defendant¿s Motion to Strike the references to attorneys fees, costs and punitive damages is sustained with leave to amend. The Motion to Strike the references to the defendants¿ liability insurance is sustained without leave to amend. This reference is improper and violated Evidence Code §1155. =(302/LMG)
